approval_request.minimum_next_approval_scope: A human must approve the exact account/profile route, profile copy, target job, proposal text, terms/payment/tax assumptions, privacy/data handling, credential policy, and anti-spam relevance before any external action.

next_local_action: If the operator approves this packet, create an exact-scope service request for one Upwork account/profile/proposal review. Do not log in, create or edit a profile, select a live job, send a proposal, message a client, accept terms, or touch payment/tax setup from this packet alone.
pre_send_checklist: Verify the profile copy contains no fake Upwork history, ratings, earnings, or client results.
Verify the proposal is for one exact job and not a generic high-volume blast.
Verify the synthetic proof asset is clearly labeled as synthetic and not a real client result.
Verify no credentials, private data, production integrations, or public posting are requested in the first proposal.
Verify Upwork account, identity, terms, fees, tax/payment, and worker-classification implications are accepted by the human operator.
Verify the first offer remains an audit/blueprint and not a production build.

draft_scope: Map one described workflow from client-provided plain-language notes.
Identify safe automation candidates and human approval points.
Produce one synthetic proof packet with no private client data.
Deliver a next-step blueprint rather than a production build.
not_in_scope: Credentials, private data, or public posting.
selected_offer.why: It uses the strongest local proof already built and aligns with public AI-agent/automation demand without needing credentials or platform action.
